Xbox ADPCM Overview

The Xbox adaptive delta pulse code modulation (ADPCM) codec allows developers to compress mono and stereo wave files at a ratio of approximately 3.5 to 1. While there are some additional considerations that you will need to keep in mind, using the Xbox ADPCM codec will allow titles to reduce the size of their audio data without the using up valuable CPU cycles.

  • Benfits
  • Considerations and Restrictions
  • Related Topics

Benefits

The following list contains some of the benefits of using Xbox ADPCM compressed wave files.

  • Decompression is handled by the Xbox Media Communications Processor (MCP).
  • Compresses audio data at a ration of approximately 3.5 to 1, which reduces the amount of memory it needs to take up.
  • Can be looped on 64-sample boundries.
  • Can be streamed.

Considerations and Restrictions

The following list contains considerations and restrictions when using Xbox ADPCM compressed wave files.

  • Loop points must be on 64-sample boundries.
  • Only supports 4 bits per sample.
  • The length of a loop must be a multiple of 64-samples.
  • Stream packets must be in physically contiguous memory (does not apply to buffers).

Related Topics

The following topics are related to Xbox ADPCM compressed wave files.

  • Installing the Xbox ADPCM Codec
  • Compressing a Wave File using the Xbox ADPCM Codec
  • Creating a DirectSound Buffer for Xbox ADPCM Compressed Waves
